findings: The plate depicts a single anatomically coherent crow: one head, one beak, two legs and feet, one tail, and a folded wing plus the opposite wing implied behind the body; the separate lower-left wing study is consistent with the bird rather than an extra appendage.

Plumage, glossy dark bill, dark eye, sturdy black legs and feet, broad fingered/pointed wing treatment, and rounded-to-square tail treatment agree with the licensed reference photos and verified Fish Crow descriptions. The bill is illustrated somewhat more massive than typical, but remains plausible.
All visible taxonomic, habitat, behavior, palette, and measurement claims are supported. The stated 36–41 cm length, 77–85 cm wingspan, and 280–350 g weight match Audubon's published range.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Fish Crow Identification — All About Birds
